name: ct_head_cerebellar_haemorrhage
clinical_history:
onset at 13:30 with left facial droop, left arm and leg weakness and slurred speech.
BP 255/161
findings:
- There is a large area of parenchymal hemorrhage in the right cerebellum measuring 5 cm in diameter with significant peri-lesional vasogenic edema and this is causing major midline shift across the horizontal cerebellar axis with complete effacement of the cerebral aqueduct and fourth ventricle and crowding of the foramen magnum due to ipsilateral tonsillar herniation

- There is some regional subarachnoid and subdural extension of haemorrhage along the tentorium

- The background brain parenchyma otherwise appears unremarkable with no gross areas of infarction

- Background chronic small vessel ischemic changes and age-appropriate involutional atrophy are noted with asymmetric predominance of the right frontal lobe

name: ct_thorax_smoker_lung_nodule
clinical_history: 68M, 40 pack-year smoker. Chronic cough and haemoptysis for 6 weeks. Referred for lung cancer workup. CT thorax with contrast for characterisation of nodule identified on recent chest X-ray.
findings:
CT thorax with IV contrast.
22 mm spiculated nodule in the right upper lobe apical segment with pleural tethering. No cavitation.
Right hilar lymphadenopathy, largest node short-axis 14 mm.
Subcarinal node, short-axis 16 mm.
No contralateral hilar adenopathy.
No pulmonary metastases.
No pleural effusion.
Background upper lobe centrilobular emphysema.
Incidental 4 mm left adrenal nodule.
Liver and imaged upper abdomen unremarkable.

name: ct_tap_acute_abdomen_gda_bleed
clinical_history: 72F, admitted 4 days ago with confusion and falls. Background AF on apixaban, T2DM, previous right MCA stroke with residual left hemiparesis. Today developed acute abdominal pain localised to epigastrium with associated vomiting. Lactate 4.2, Hb dropped from 112 to 87 over 24 hours. Query mesenteric ischaemia, query GI bleed source, query intra-abdominal pathology. Previous imaging includes CT head on admission (old infarct only) and an abdominal ultrasound 6 months ago (fatty liver, small abdominal aortic aneurysm 3.8cm infrarenal).
findings:
Scan performed thorax abdomen pelvis portal venous phase, no arterial phase obtained
Large volume haemoperitoneum throughout abdomen and pelvis, highest density in pelvis and around liver
Active contrast extravasation from gastroduodenal artery branch, approximately 2cm distal to origin, forming a small pseudoaneurysm approximately 8mm
Duodenum D1/D2 shows wall thickening and mural gas, adjacent fat stranding
Infrarenal abdominal aortic aneurysm now measures 4.6cm, previously 3.8cm on US 6 months ago, no evidence of rupture or contained leak
Pancreas appears atrophic with pancreatic duct dilation to 6mm, calcifications within pancreatic head, consistent with chronic pancreatitis
Multiple small hypodensities in both kidneys, largest 1.2cm right interpolar, not fully characterised on this phase
Large hiatus hernia with approximately 40% of stomach intrathoracic
Bilateral dependent atelectasis, small right pleural effusion
Left adrenal thickening, nodular, 2.4cm, not previously characterised
Cholelithiasis, no wall thickening
Moderate bilateral pelvic free fluid continuous with the haemoperitoneum
Old right MCA territory encephalomalacia visible on scout images only
Bladder partially distended, catheter not yet placed
Diffuse colonic faecal loading
Background mild-moderate atherosclerosis
